creator: barbara.xxx1975@libero.it
text:
My laptop crashed while copying from it some files using scp.
If it could help, pciconf reports the following about the NIC.

em0@pci0:0:25:0:        class=0x020000 card=0x20ee17aa chip=0x10f58086 rev=0x03 hdr=0x00
    vendor     = 'Intel Corporation'
    device     = 'Intel  82567LM-2 Gigabit Network Connection (82567LM)'
    class      = network
    subclass   = ethernet



Unread portion of the kernel message buffer:
panic: vm_fault: fault on nofault entry, addr: c10a5000
cpuid = 1
Uptime: 1d1h12m49s
Physical memory: 1981 MB
Dumping 256 MB: 241 225 209 193 177 161 145 129 113 97 81 65 49 33 17 1

Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/acpi_ibm.ko...Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/acpi_ibm.ko.symbols...done.
done.
Loaded symbols for /boot/kernel/acpi_ibm.ko
Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/linprocfs.ko...Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/linprocfs.ko.symbols...done.
done.
Loaded symbols for /boot/kernel/linprocfs.ko
Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/linux.ko...Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/linux.ko.symbols...done.
done.
Loaded symbols for /boot/kernel/linux.ko
Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/logo_saver.ko...Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/logo_saver.ko.symbols...done.
done.
Loaded symbols for /boot/kernel/logo_saver.ko

246     pcpu.h: No such file or directory.
        in pcpu.h
